package.name: Tutorials
package.description: Learn and explore Datagrok through interactive tutorials and a demo app

functions.name: Demo
functions.description: Interactive demo of major Datagrok capabilities

functions.name: filesDemo
functions.description: The File Manager is an interface that allows you to manage connections, browse and preview file content, and perform standard file and folder actions such as opening, downloading, deleting, and renaming.

functions.name: databasesDemo
functions.description: Database Manager provides a hierarchical browsing interface for schemas and database objects, such as queries, tables, and table columns (if supported by the providers). You can perform various operations like adding…

functions.name: scatterPlotDemo
functions.description: A scatter plot (also called a scatter graph, scatter chart, scattergram, or scatter diagram) is a type of plot or mathematical diagram using Cartesian coordinates to display values for typically two variables for a set…

functions.name: histogramDemo
functions.description: A histogram is a graphical representation of the distribution of numerical data.

functions.name: lineChartDemo
functions.description: A line chart is a simple, two-dimensional chart with an X and Y axis, each point representing a single value. The data points are joined by a line to depict a trend, usually over time.

functions.name: barChartDemo
functions.description: A bar chart presents grouped data as rectangular bars with lengths proportional to the values that they represent. Unlike histograms which you can apply to display the distribution of numerical data, bar charts are…

functions.name: pieChartDemo
functions.description: A pie chart is useful for reflecting numerical proportions. Conceptually, it is similar to a bar chart in that it represents categorical values. A pie chart shows the relative size of a given category (a slice of the…

functions.name: trellisPlotDemo
functions.description: Trellis Charts are useful for finding the structure and patterns in complex data. A Trellis Chart is a layout of smaller charts in a grid with consistent scales. Each smaller chart represents rows that belong to a…

functions.name: matrixPlotDemo
functions.description: Use a Matrix Plot to assess the relationship among many pairs of columns at the same time.

functions.name: scatterPlot3DDemo
functions.description: Use a 3D scatter plot to plot data points on three axes to show the relationship between three variables. Each row in the data table is represented by a marker whose position depends on its values in the columns set on…

functions.name: densityPlotDemo
functions.description: Unlike a scatter plot that visualizes each individual data point, the density plot splits 2D area by bins and color-codes it depending on the number of points that fall within this bin. The darker the color, the more…

functions.name: pcPlotDemo
functions.description: Parallel coordinates are a common way of visualizing high-dimensional geometry and analyzing multivariate data. To show a set of points in an n-dimensional space, a backdrop is drawn consisting of n parallel lines,…

functions.name: networkDiagramDemo
functions.description: A network diagram is used to visualize graphs, where values of the specified two columns become nodes, and rows become edges. It is possible to color-code and size-code nodes and columns by choosing the aggregate…

functions.name: boxPlotDemo
functions.description: The box plot (a.k.a. box and whisker diagram) is a standardized way of displaying the distribution of data based on the five-number summary: minimum, first quartile, median, third quartile, and maximum.

functions.name: treeMapDemo
functions.description: Treemap displays hierarchical (tree-structured) data as nested rectangles. The branches are rectangles, then tiled with smaller rectangles representing sub-branches. Rectangles have areas proportional to a specified…

functions.name: statisticsDemo
functions.description: Provides specified descriptive statistics for the chosen columns.

functions.name: correlationPlotDemo
functions.description: A quick way to assess correlations between all columns at once. Cells are color-coded by the Pearson correlation coefficient or Spearman's rank correlation coefficient. Histograms along the diagonal show the…

functions.name: calendarDemo
functions.description: Calendar lets you analyze longitudinal data. It needs at least one column of type DateTime.

functions.name: gridDemo
functions.description: A grid table contains a set of data that is structured in rows and columns. It allows the user to scroll in both directions and can handle large numbers of items and columns.

functions.name: markupDemo
functions.description: Use this viewer to host any text, arbitrary HTML content, or markdown-formatted text. In most cases, the viewer will auto-detect content type. Use the 'mode' property to explicitly specify it.

functions.name: tileViewerDemo
functions.description: Visualizes rows as a collection of forms that are positioned as tiles.

functions.name: formDemo
functions.description: Form allows you to customize the appearance of the row by manually positioning the fields, and adding other visual elements, such as pictures or panels. A form can be used either as a stand-alone viewer or as a row…

functions.name: pivotTableDemo
functions.description: A pivot table is a table of grouped values that aggregates the individual items of a more extensive table within one or more discrete categories. This summary might include sums, averages, or other statistics, which the…

functions.name: filtersDemo
functions.description: Filter is a set of controls for quick filtering, selection, and visual assessment of column values.

functions.name: tableLinkingDemo
functions.description: Table linking is based on the key columns. The link type determines which records should be synchronized between the datasets (current record, filter, selection, and mouse-over record).

tutorials.name: Predictive Modeling
tutorials.description: Predictive modeling is a statistical technique used to predict outcomes based on historical data.

tutorials.name: Scripting
tutorials.description: Scripting is an integration mechanism with languages for statistical computing

tutorials.name: Filters
tutorials.description: A set of controls for quick filtering, selection,  and visual assessment of column values

tutorials.name: Scatter Plot
tutorials.description: A graph in which the values of two variables are plotted along two axes

tutorials.name: Viewers
tutorials.description: Learn how to use different viewers together

tutorials.name: Data Connectors
tutorials.description: Direct connection to data sources and databases using the connector server

tutorials.name: Differential equations
tutorials.description: Learn how to model processes defined by ordinary differential equations with Diff Studio

tutorials.name: Parameter optimization
tutorials.description: Find the initial conditions that produce a desired model output

tutorials.name: Sensitivity analysis
tutorials.description: Analyze the relationship between inputs and outputs of the model

tutorials.name: Activity Cliffs
tutorials.description: The Activity Cliffs tool detects and visualizes pairs of molecules  with highly similar structures but significantly different activity levels.  It uses distance-based dimensionality reduction algorithms  to convert cross-similarities into a 2D…

tutorials.name: R-Groups Analysis
tutorials.description: R-Groups Analysis lets you identify all R-group  variations around a scaffold, analyze substitution patterns, evaluate their  impact on crucial compound properties, and find gaps.

tutorials.name: Similarity and Diversity Search
tutorials.description: You can analyze a collection of molecules based on molecular similarity.  Similarity Search finds structures similar to the reference molecule,  while Diversity Search shows N molecules of different chemical classes in the dataset.

tutorials.name: Substructure Search and Filtering
tutorials.description: You can interactively explore datasets using filters.  Specifically for molecules, Datagrok uses integrated sketchers to filter by substructure.

tutorials.name: Virtual Screening
tutorials.description: In this tutorial, you are playing the role of an in silico researcher.  The department of chemical synthesis has provided a number of new compounds, and biological  laboratories measured their in vitro activities against one of the molecular…

changelog.text: Added demo image for Retrosynthesis
GROK-17970: Demo app no longer closes user-opened content when running demos
GROK-19746: Demo app browse-toolbox no longer flickers when switching between demos
Tutorial: Updated the scientific computing tutorials
Tutorial: Updated the parameter optimization tutorial
Fitting: New layout related update
GROK-18139: Activity dashboard: Retired Learn widget and added it to the activity dashboard instead
Demo app: Fixed the issue that names with `-` in name sometimes can't open
GROK-18767: Fixed opening Demo in learn widget opens empty toolbox
Fixed Demo > Cheminformatics is opened even for old users
GROK-18139: Added demo app hierarchy as function in Tutorials
GROK-18718: Expanded Demo > Cheminformatics by default for new users
GROK-18879: Fixed Data aggregation tutorial
GROK-17845: Fixed each click opens additional app menu in Tutorials widget
GROK-18791: Fixed toolbox isn't shown in Scatterplot tutorial
Added Peptides tutorial
Dashboards tutorial: Fixed UI inconsistency in names
GROK-17832: Fixes in sync with new Browse UX
Fixed the Parameters Optimization tutorial
Updated UI in the Multivariate Analysis tutorial
Fixed the behavior of hints in the Parameters Optimization tutorial
Updated the Parameters Optimization tutorial
Updated Demos
Updated the Scientific Computing tutorials with respect to new Model Hub UI
Updated demo features and the Scientific Computing tutorials with respect to new Browse UX
Added scientific computing tutorials:
Differential equations
Sensitivity analysis
Parameters optimization
Fixed Cheminformatics tutorials
